name: Malachai, the Cruel Messenger
description:
Malachai, the Cruel Messenger

[colback=black!5,colframe=red!70!black,title=Content Warning,breakable] Malachai is a patron of curses, corruption, and Faustian bargains. Themes include addiction, loss of self, and the consequences of desperate choices. While the mechanics focus on supernatural afflictions, the fiction may touch on dark subjects. Please use Lines & Veils to ensure everyone's comfort at the table.

*{Lore "She gave us everything we wanted but we did not ask the price." ---The Worn Magi

Malachai was once a divine messenger---a herald of painful truths, sent to deliver news that could not be softened. She was not cruel; she was necessary. But necessity wears. In time, she began to soften the truth, offering false hope alongside real curses. For this, the gods bound her in chains of black iron beneath the cursed city of Zahk'Kozahn.

Now she waits. Chained but not contained. Imprisoned but not silent. Her voice reaches across the world, offering power to the weak, hope to the hopeless, and sweet oblivion to those who cannot bear to face the dawn. Vampirism, lycanthropy, and other cursed conditions flow from her influence. Her followers learn that power demands suffering, mastering gifts that devour from within.

Among the peoples of Fate's Edge, Malachai is the patron of the desperate and the damned. In Zakov, she is the Salt-Fang, whose silver coin appears in the mouth of drowned sailors. In the Valewood, she is the Thorn-Giver, invoked by hunters who curse a rival's bow. In Linn, she is the Wave-Mother's Shadow, whose gifts turn the tide of battle---at a price. Wherever hope curdles into despair, Malachai whispers.

quote "I offer you everything you desire, and everything you fear. The payment comes later." quote

*{Domain Focus itemize Cursed Gifts: Faustian bargains, power with hidden costs Corruptive Power: tainted blessings, addictive magic False Salvation: hope that destroys, beautiful lies Supernatural Afflictions: vampirism, lycanthropy, and other curses itemize

*{Thiasos and Codex (Runekeeper Options)

A Runekeeper sworn to Malachai does not bind a familiar of purity or light. Their Thiasos is a small creature touched by her corruption: a black rat with one red eye that gnaws at the bars of its cage, a crow with a broken feather that always points toward the nearest corpse, a toad that secretes a toxin causing fever dreams, a small bat that drinks blood from a silver dish, or a moth whose wing dust leaves a rash that itches for a week. This creature requires no food, but it must be given a small sacrifice of pain (a pinprick, a pinch of salt in a wound) each day. If neglected for a week, it becomes Compromised (it grows sullen and its aura of despair thickens, imposing --1 die on all Resolve rolls for its bearer until a proper curse is laid).

The Codex of Malachai is never a book of hope. It may be a chain of iron links, each etched with a curse; a box of cracked bells that toll when a Rite is spoken; a scroll of skin from a sinner who died unshriven; or a set of silver nails driven into a board, each nail a promise broken. Because Malachai's truths are written in pain, the Codex requires upkeep: the Runekeeper must spend an hour each week pricking their finger and letting a drop of blood fall onto each nail (or each bell), or the curses stored there become faint (treat as Compromised until re-blooded).

*{Patron's Gift (Imbuement)

Once per scene as an action, a Runekeeper of Malachai may touch a held item (weapon, tool, or garment) and whisper a false promise. For the rest of the scene, the item grants +1 die to Intimidation or Deception rolls, and the first time each scene the bearer would suffer a supernatural curse, they may ignore it (the curse slides off, seeking another target). After the scene, the user marks +1 Obligation to Malachai.
summary: Malachai, the Cruel Messenger [colback=black!5,colframe=red!70!black,title=Content Warning,breakable] Malachai is a patron of curses, corruption, and Faustian bargains. Themes include addiction, loss of self, and the consequences of desperate choices.

rites.name: Rite of the Honeyed Binding
rites.description: (Basic, 5 XP) Scene; Self or Near/Touch; Yes. Tags: , , , Materials: Blood mixed with sweet wine, or a broken chain link. Effect: Choose one: itemize Honeyed Curse: Target gains +2 dice to their next roll (counts as Triumph). They take 1 Corruption (unresistable). Binding Curse: Target gains +1 die to physical actions but suffers Fatigue 1 unless they perform a dominance act (beat a weaker creature, drink blood, break something precious). itemize Push It: (Honeyed) Grant +3 dice and auto-Triumph; inflict 2 Corruption instead. (Binding) Grant +2 dice but Fatigue 2; mark 1 SB (Spades) as the curse's hunger grows. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Choose one: itemize Honeyed Curse: Target gains +2 dice to their next roll (counts as Triumph). They take 1 Corruption (unresistable). Binding Curse: Target gains +1 die to physical actions but suffers Fatigue 1 unless they perform a dominance act (beat a weaker creature, drink blood, break something precious). itemize

rites.name: Rite of the False Dawn
rites.description: (Advanced, 8 XP) Scene; Near; Yes. Tags: , , Materials: Meteoric iron shard blessed by false light. Effect: Bestow a gift with a hidden curse. Target gains a significant advantage (e.g., +1 die to all rolls for one scene) but accrues a 4-segment Corruption timer. Each time they use the gift, tick the timer. When full, they suffer a related curse (GM chooses: festering wounds, beastly urges, or the attention of a Collector). Push It: The gift is more powerful (+2 dice, or extended duration). The timer advances 2 segments immediately; mark 1 SB (Diamonds). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Bestow a gift with a hidden curse. Target gains a significant advantage (e.g., +1 die to all rolls for one scene) but accrues a 4-segment Corruption timer. Each time they use the gift, tick the timer. When full, they suffer a related curse (GM chooses: festering wounds, beastly urges, or the attention of a Collector). \\

rites.name: Rite of the Cruel Transformation
rites.description: (Advanced, 9 XP) Scene; Touch; Yes. Tags: , , , Materials: Holy water mixed with graveyard dust. Effect: Infect a target with a supernatural condition (vampiric hunger, bestial rage, shadow-bind). Lasts one scene. Start a 6-segment Transformation timer. Each time the target resists feeding or transformation, tick the timer. When full, the condition becomes permanent. Push It: The condition becomes permanent immediately; target gains +1 die to relevant actions but is marked by Malachai (a Collector will visit within a week). Mark 1 SB (Hearts). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Infect a target with a supernatural condition (vampiric hunger, bestial rage, shadow-bind). Lasts one scene. Start a 6-segment Transformation timer. Each time the target resists feeding or transformation, tick the timer. When full, the condition becomes permanent. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Messenger's Burden
rites.description: (Master, 13 XP) Extended; Self; Yes. Tags: , , , Materials: Manacles worn while speaking her name. Effect: Channel Malachai directly. Gain +2 dice to one action per beat this scene, but each use marks 1 SB as her influence spreads. Start an 8-segment Corruption's Grip timer. When it fills, a Collector arrives to demand a memory, a name, or a year of your life. Push It: +3 dice but mark 2 SB, suffer Fatigue 1, and her voice becomes permanent---you hear whispers of cruel bargains in quiet moments. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: Extended action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Channel Malachai directly. Gain +2 dice to one action per beat this scene, but each use marks 1 SB as her influence spreads. Start an 8-segment Corruption's Grip timer. When it fills, a Collector arrives to demand a memory, a name, or a year of your life. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Chained Angel's Tribute
rites.description: (Master, 14 XP) Extended; Zone; No. Tags: , , , Materials: A chapel bell cracked by divine lightning, or a silver coin that cannot be spent. Effect: Corrupt a sacred space or consecrate a site to Malachai. Within the zone: itemize Supernatural effects (curses, dark magic) gain +1 Effect. Blessings and healing spells generate +1 SB on a failure. Any creature that accepts a gift from Malachai within the zone immediately ticks a Corruption timer twice. itemize Start a 10-segment Divine Perversion timer. When it fills, the site becomes a permanent beacon for the Worn---the ghouls of Zahk'Kozahn. Push It: The corruption spreads to adjacent areas; mark 3 SB (Diamonds). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +4) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +3 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: Extended action. Duration: Permanent.
rites.effect: Corrupt a sacred space or consecrate a site to Malachai. Within the zone: itemize Supernatural effects (curses, dark magic) gain +1 Effect. Blessings and healing spells generate +1 SB on a failure. Any creature that accepts a gift from Malachai within the zone immediately ticks a Corruption timer twice. itemize Start a 10-segment Divine Perversion timer. When it fills, the site becomes a permanent beacon for the Worn---the ghouls of Zahk'Kozahn. \\
